one_line_ask: Grade four raw burger photos, vectorize the Brickyard wordmark, source a chalkboard texture, and run the client's authored InDesign menu template through a data-merge to produce a print-ready PDF and high-res JPEG.
brief: Brickyard, a retro street-food burger joint, needs its one-page menu finished for print. The owner has handed off an authored InDesign menu template (menu_template.indd) laid out in a rustic chalkboard style with four photo frames and real data-merge fields (<<section>>, <<item>>, <<desc>>, <<price>> across Classic Burgers, Specialty Burgers, Sides, Beverages), the brand wordmark as a flat PNG, four raw phone-shot food photos (a double Brickyard, a specialty bacon-jalapeño burger, a basket of fries, a craft cola), and a CSV of placeholder item names / descriptions / prices. The four food photos are unappetizing as-shot: slightly tilted, dull/flat, cool white-balance, busy backgrounds. The job: (1) make every food photo look vibrant and appetizing — straighten, auto-tone, warm the white balance toward a hot street-food look, push vibrance, apply a food-pop preset, and throw the cluttered background out of focus so the food reads as hero; consistently crop all four to the same frame ratio the template expects; (2) lift the hero double-smash shot cleanly onto a transparent background so it can sit over the chalkboard without a hard photographic edge; (3) vectorize the flat wordmark PN
